plan.title: Document the headless / low-ceremony launch path in the swarm skill

narratives.Description: Documents the low-ceremony headless launch path in the swarm skill so operators with a pre-approved cohort can bypass the interactive promote-fill loop and per-phase approval gates. It updates Phase 0, Phase 2, and Phase 3 prose to route through the deterministic launch engine, accept pre-created worktrees, and consume the launch manifest, and mirrors the gate-stack note in AGENTS.md. A content test pins the prose contract so the behavior is observable and regression-guarded.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Extend skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 0 with a headless cohort entry that batches the approval gates into a single consent and routes through the task swarm:launch CLI, and Phase 2 to accept a pre-created worktree map.
2. Document Phase 3 launch-manifest consumption and mirror the gate-stack note in AGENTS.md so both surfaces agree.
3. Add tests/content/test_swarm_headless_launch.py asserting the headless prose contract is present in both the skill and AGENTS.md.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want the skill to document a headless launch path, so that I can run a ready cohort with a single consent instead of many gates.

narrative.Acceptance: When the swarm skill renders Phase 0 for a pre-approved cohort, it documents skipping the interactive promote-fill loop and routing through task swarm:launch.
When the swarm skill renders Phase 2, it documents accepting a pre-created worktree map instead of always creating worktrees itself.
When the swarm skill renders Phase 3, it documents consuming the launch-manifest emitted by task swarm:launch before dispatch.
When the content test runs, it asserts the headless launch entry is present in both the swarm skill and the AGENTS.md mirror.

references.title: Issue #1387: feat(swarm): low-ceremony / headless launch for pre-approved cohorts (build on #1378 allocation-context token)
